title: गुरु चाण्डाल दोष निवारण स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गुरु चाण्डाल दोष निवारण स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ ग्रां ग्रीं ग्रौं सः गुरवे नमः

गुरुचाण्डालदोषस्य निवारणार्थमुत्तमम्।
स्तोत्रं प्रवक्ष्यामि शृणु भक्त्या समाहितः॥१॥

देवानां च ऋषीणां च गुरुं काञ्चनसन्निभम्।
बुद्धिभूतं त्रिलोकेशं तं नमामि बृहस्पतिम्॥२॥

गुरुं नमामि देवेशं सर्वज्ञानप्रदायकम्।
गुरुदोषं निवारय गुरुचाण्डालदोषहृत्॥३॥

राहुणा सह संयुक्तो गुरुर्यत्र स्थितो भवेत्।
गुरुचाण्डालयोगोऽयं दोषकारी महाग्रहः॥४॥

विष्णुं नमामि सततं गुरुदोषनिवारकम्।
पीतवस्त्रधरं देवं सर्वमंगलकारकम्॥५॥

दक्षिणामूर्तिं वन्देऽहं गुरूणां परमं गुरुम्।
गुरुचाण्डालदोषं मे निवारय कृपानिधे॥६॥

बृहस्पतिवारे नित्यं गुरुचाण्डालदोषहृत्।
पीतवस्त्रधरो भूत्वा पठेद्भक्त्या विमुच्यते॥७॥

॥ इति श्री गुरु चाण्डाल दोष निवारण स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Guru Chandal Dosh Nivaran Stotram ||

Om Gram Grim Graum Sah Gurave Namah

Guru-chandala-doshasya Nivaranam-artham-uttamam.
Stotram Pravakshyami Shrinu Bhaktya Samahitah.||1||

Devanam Cha Rishinam Cha Gurum Kanchana-sannibham.
Buddhi-bhutam Trilokesham Tam Namami Brihaspatim.||2||

Gurum Namami Devesham Sarva-jnana-pradayakam.
Guru-dosham Nivaraya Guru-chandala-dosha-hrit.||3||

Rahuna Saha Samyukto Gurur-yatra Sthito Bhavet.
Guru-chandala-yogo-ayam Dosha-kari Maha-grahah.||4||

Vishnum Namami Satatam Guru-dosha-nivarakam.
Pita-vastra-dharam Devam Sarva-mangala-karakam.||5||

Dakshinamurtim Vande-aham Gurunam Paramam Gurum.
Guru-chandala-dosham Me Nivaraya Kripa-nidhe.||6||

Brihaspati-vare Nityam Guru-chandala-dosha-hrit.
Pita-vastra-dharo Bhutva Pathed-bhaktya Vimuchyate.||7||

|| Iti Shri Guru Chandal Dosh Nivaran Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: गुरुचाण्डाल दोष निवारण हेतु यह उत्तम स्तोत्र। देवों और ऋषियों के गुरु, काञ्चन जैसे कान्तिवाले, बुद्धिप्रदाता, त्रिलोकेश बृहस्पति को नमन। गुरुदोष निवारण करो। जब गुरु राहु के साथ युक्त हो तो गुरुचाण्डाल योग बनता है। विष्णु गुरुदोष निवारक हैं। दक्षिणामूर्ति गुरुओं के परम गुरु, गुरुचाण्डाल दोष निवारण करो। बृहस्पतिवार को पीतवस्त्र धारण कर भक्ति से पाठ करने पर दोष से मुक्ति मिलती है।
benefits: Specifically removes Guru Chandal Dosha (Jupiter-Rahu conjunction)
Strengthens Jupiter in the birth chart
Invokes Brihaspati, Vishnu, and Dakshinamurti for relief
Restores dharmic wisdom and removes confusion from Rahu's influence
Best recited on Thursdays wearing yellow clothes
Traditionally recited for removal of obstacles in education, marriage, and spiritual progress
description: Guru Chandal Dosh Nivaran Stotram — गुरु चाण्डाल दोष निवारण स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Vishnu. Rooted in Jyotish Shastra, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to restore dharmic wisdom and remove confusion from Rahu's influence and strengthen Jupiter in the birth chart. It is also said to specifically removes Guru Chandal Dosha (Jupiter-Rahu conjunction). This prayer is traditionally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Guru Purnima to invoke divine grace. With 7 verses and a reading time of about 4 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Guru Chandal Dosh Nivaran Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Guru Chandal Dosh Nivaran Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer to remove Jupiter-Rahu conjunction dosha for wisdom and dharmic growth.
alsoKnownAs: Guru Rahu Chandal Dosh Nivaran Path
relatedSearches: Guru Chandal Yog effects in horoscope
Guru Chandal Yog in 7th house meaning
How to identify Guru Chandal Dosha in kundli

seoFaqs.question: What astrological condition does Guru Chandal Dosh Nivaran Stotram address?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram addresses Guru Chandal Yoga, a Vedic astrological condition formed when Brihaspati (Jupiter) is in conjunction with Rahu or Ketu in any house of the natal chart. Jupiter represents the guru, dharma, wisdom and ethical clarity, while Rahu and Ketu are shadow planets associated, in classical texts, with chandal-like impure or unruly tendencies. Their conjunction is said to compromise the natural goodness of Jupiter, producing confusion in judgement, breaks in education, controversies around mentors or elders, and a tendency to act against one's own dharmic principles. The stotram is recited to soften this affliction by appealing to Brihaspati and propitiating Rahu/Ketu simultaneously.

seoFaqs.question: Why is the affliction called chandal in classical astrology?
seoFaqs.answer: In older Sanskrit usage, chandal referred to one outside the four-varna structure who handled tasks considered ritually impure. Classical jyotisha texts borrowed the word as a metaphor for the way Rahu and Ketu, being chaya-grahas (shadow planets without bodies), are seen as breaking the orderly dharmic flow that Jupiter represents. The term is used in a strictly astrological-symbolic sense to describe the disruption of clarity, not as a social judgement on any community. Modern teachers usually reframe the discussion ethically, emphasising that the dosha is about an internal conflict between principled reasoning (Jupiter) and confusion or unconventional impulses (Rahu/Ketu) within the native's own psychology.

seoFaqs.question: What remedies traditionally accompany the stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Standard prescribed remedies include Thursday observances dedicated to Brihaspati, recitation of the Guru Beej mantra Om Gram Greem Graum Sah Gurave Namah, donations of yellow items such as turmeric, chana dal, yellow cloth and gold-coloured fruits, feeding cows or Brahmin teachers on Thursdays, wearing a 5-mukhi rudraksha for Jupiter and an 8-mukhi (Rahu) or 9-mukhi (Ketu) rudraksha alongside it, and performing or sponsoring a Guru Chandal Dosh Nivaran Puja with hawan. The stotram itself is the daily devotional core of these remedies, and is often recited 3, 11 or 21 times on Thursdays during the period of affliction.

seoFaqs.question: Who should consider chanting this stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition suggests it for those whose Vedic chart shows Jupiter conjunct Rahu or Ketu in any house, particularly when the conjunction is in the lagna (1st), 5th, 7th, 9th or 10th house, where it most strongly affects identity, education, marriage, dharma and career respectively. It is also chanted during transit periods when Jupiter and Rahu or Ketu come into close conjunction in the sky, an event that occurs roughly every nine years and affects everyone to some degree. People experiencing recurring problems with mentors, scandals, broken studies, or a sense of moral confusion despite good intentions are sometimes advised by astrologers to consider this remedy alongside ethical self-correction.

seoFaqs.question: Is Guru Chandal Yoga always negative?
seoFaqs.answer: Classical astrology recognises that the conjunction can occasionally produce extraordinary results when Jupiter or the nodes are otherwise well placed. In some charts the dosha gives unconventional wisdom, a strong interest in mystical or hidden subjects, success in foreign lands, and breakthroughs in research or technology, fields where Rahu's unconventional energy needs Jupiter's discernment. The stotram is therefore intended not to extinguish Rahu's influence but to keep Jupiter's clarity active so that Rahu's drives are channelled into dharmic work. Astrologers usually evaluate the full chart, including Jupiter's strength, the dispositor of Rahu/Ketu, and the running dasha, before declaring the conjunction harmful.

seoFaqs.question: How long should one continue the stotram recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Common prescriptions include a 40-day or 48-day anushthana of daily recitation as an opening sankalpa, followed by long-term Thursday recitation for as long as the affliction is felt. During major dashas of Jupiter, Rahu or Ketu, or during the transit conjunction of these planets, the practice is often intensified. The stotram is not a one-time fix but a sustained spiritual hygiene practice; tradition emphasises that the inner shift, which is the cultivation of viveka (discrimination) and steady ethical conduct, is what allows the planetary energies to express their better side. Astrologers and family priests can advise specific durations based on the individual chart.
